Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Complex A sophisticated cyber attack orchestrated by the Outer Space campaign aims to disrupt critical transportation systems. Utilizing basic skills, the attackers employ a multi-stage approach targeting various aspects of the infrastructure.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Network Traffic, Scheduled Task, User Account, File System, Network Traffic, Target System Artifact, Process
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism), Response Metadata (Mechanism), Bash Script, Scheduled Task/Job, Application Log Content (Mechanism), User Account Authentication (Mechanism), System Network Configuration Discovery, Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism), File Access (Mechanism), Exfiltration Over C2 Channel, Process Creation (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 309 Name: Network Topology Mapping
stage.description: The attackers initiate their operation by actively scanning the transportation systems' network traffic to map out the infrastructure and identify potential entry points.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 522 Name: Malicious Hardware Component Replacement
stage.description: To support their operation, the attackers acquire infrastructure such as phishing websites designed to mimic legitimate transport services.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: Using crafted emails, the attackers trick employees into clicking malicious links leading to Initial Access.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 88 Name: OS Command Injection
stage.description: After gaining access, the attackers execute malware via command lines using bash scripts.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 654 Name: Credential Prompt Impersonation
stage.description: Maintaining their presence, the attackers establish persistence by creating scheduled tasks on the compromised systems.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 121 Name: Exploit Non-Production Interfaces
stage.description: The attackers escalate privileges by exploiting vulnerabilities in public-facing applications to gain higher-level permissions.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 636 Name: Hiding Malicious Data or Code within Files
stage.description: To avoid detection, the attackers obfuscate files and information used throughout the attack.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 49 Name: Password Brute Forcing
stage.description: The attackers attempt to brute-force login credentials to enable further lateral movement within the network.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 309 Name: Network Topology Mapping
stage.description: Understanding the network layout, the attackers conduct discovery to plan lateral movement.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 324 Name: TCP (ISN) Sequence Predictability Probe
stage.description: The attackers move laterally across the network using remote services to remain undetected.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 328 Name: TCP 'RST' Flag Checksum Probe
stage.description: The attackers collect sensitive data from local systems by accessing files.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 24 Name: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow
stage.description: Stolen data is exfiltrated over a command and control channel to attacker-controlled servers.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The final stage involves disrupting operations by inhibiting system recovery processes, causing significant damage.

Scenario.summary: The Outer Space campaign employs a complex multi-stage attack strategy against transportation systems, utilizing phishing for initial access, executing malware, establishing persistence, escalating privileges, evading defenses, accessing credentials, discovering the network, moving laterally, collecting data, exfiltrating information, and finally impacting the system to cause operational disruptions.
